zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Stafford fitzPiers於1166年出生在英國林可爾史爾時，她的父親Galfridus FitzPiers23歲，母親Maud Stafford16歲。她和Ralph de Sudeley至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1246年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾素德勒享年80歲。

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Stafford fitzPiers was born in 1166, in Lincolnshire, England, her father, Galfridus FitzPiers, was 23 and her mother, Maud Stafford, was 16. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Ralph de Sudeley. She died in 1246, in Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Stafford fitzPiers est née en 1166, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Galfridus FitzPiers, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Maud Stafford, avait 16 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Ralph de Sudeley. Elle est décédée en 1246, à Sudeley, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Stafford fitzPiers 1166 in Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Galfridus FitzPiers 23 und ihre Mutter Maud Stafford 16. Sie hatte mit Ralph de Sudeley mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1246 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Stafford fitzPiers è nata il nel 1166 nel Lincolnshire, England, sua padre, Galfridus FitzPiers, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Maud Stafford, aveva 16 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Ralph de Sudeley. Morì nel 1246, nel Sudeley, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 80 anni.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Stafford fitzPiers родилась в 1166 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, её отцу по имени Galfridus FitzPiers было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Stafford, 16.
